title: Halimium atriplicifolium
taxonomy: Halimium atriplicifolium is classified within the family Cistaceae. This plant is a member of the genus Halimium, which is native to the Mediterranean region. The classification of Halimium atriplicifolium further breaks down into the kingdom Plantae, with its species rank being a succulent perennial herb. The classification is based on its morphological characteristics and molecular analysis. It is further grouped into the order Rosales and the clade core eudicots.

intro: Halimium atriplicifolium, also known as Rockrose, is a perennial succulent shrub belonging to the Cistaceae family. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including skin conditions such as eczema and acne, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Halimium atriplicifolium include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal preparations, Rockrose is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures to support skin health and alleviate symptoms of stress and anxiety.
